description: Using a different analyzer at query time than at index time, so obviously-present terms return zero hits.
body: Failure mode: the query is analyzed differently (or not at all) compared to the indexed field, so their tokens never match and a term clearly present in documents returns no results. Detection signals: an exact-looking query yields zero hits while the document visibly contains the term; the analyze API shows different tokens for index vs query; case or stemming differences. Do not assume zero hits means missing data. Safer fallback: align the search_analyzer with the index analyzer, confirm token alignment via the analyze API, and re-query.
